title: Bookbot Outcomes, Repository Baseline, and Evidence · Transfer Build
summary: An independent archive team receives a different authorized corpus, platform, language mix, file size, report consumer, terminal width, error policy, and delivery constraint and transfers bookbot outcomes, repository baseline, and evidence into a changed Bookbot outcome, corpus, repository, and evidence charter. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of Bookbot outcome, corpus, repository, and evidence charter with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Define the report user, decision, input corpus, output contract, consequence of error, and observable success before coding.
Record text owner, license or permission, source, version, checksum, language, and allowed use before analysis.
Inspect Git status, branch, remotes, configuration origin, tracked files, ignore rules, and baseline tests before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab User Decision
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Bookbot team handles case 7895 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the Bookbot outcome on user need, corpus authority, repository state, and changed-case evidence; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define the report user, decision, input corpus, output contract, consequence of error, and observable success before coding.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-evidence-ladder-pojudg" that uses case 4152 to gate the Bookbot outcome on user need, corpus authority, repository state, and changed-case evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Evidence Ladder.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Evidence Ladder into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Evidence Ladder, keep Evidence Ladder connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-evidence-ladder-pojudg
# Competency: bookbot-evidence-ladder.
# Case 4152: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy.
def evidence_bookbot_evidence_ladder_pojudg(user: str = "archive-reader", authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 6, repository_clean: bool = True, changed_case_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_pojudg = "pojudg-gdujop-4152"
    assert evidence_variant_pojudg
    evidence_axes_pojudg = (
        "corpuspojudg",
        "policygdujop",
        "revision4152",
        "commandpojudg4152",
        "reportgdujop4152",
        "test4152pojudg",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_pojudg)) == 6
    if not user.strip() or acceptance_cases < 1:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "corpus-authority"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not changed_case_kept:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "evidence-gap"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 1
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-charter"
